entry.title: Taipei Times: 3 Aug, 2022

entry.title: Washington Post: 2 Aug, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Ukraine Live Briefing: At least 140,000 residential buildings destroyed, 3.5 million Ukrainians homeless, Ukraine says
  • Zawahiri’s legacy: Querulous bands of militants and a chance for rebirth
  • Live updates: Nancy Pelosi in Taiwan as China says House speaker ‘playing with fire’
  • White House scrambles to avoid crisis amid Pelosi visit to Taiwan
  • Wanted: A taste-tester willing to try 3,500 pieces of candy per month
  • Pelosi to meet with Taiwan’s biggest semiconductor manufacturer
  • U.S. imposes sanctions on Putin’s reported girlfriend
  • Taliban facing backlash after U.S. drone strike against al-Qaeda leader
  • What’s at stake with Pelosi’s visit to Taiwan
  • Iraq crisis simmers as protesters shut parliament down
  • BP profits highest in 14 years, raking in $8.5 billion amid gas pump pinch

  • Pelosi visit is a test for Taiwan’s global status under Chinese pressure

  • World is ‘one miscalculation away from nuclear annihilation,’ U.N. says
  • Brittney Griner back in Russian court amid hopes of a prisoner swap
  • What Ayman al-Zawahiri’s killing means for al-Qaeda
  • U.S. agency warns of Afghan famine, more suppression of women’s rights
  • Ukraine Live Briefing: First grain shipment leaves Ukraine as Zelensky urges security for future food exports
  • Mexico's attorney general investigating ex-president
  • US seeks possession of Venezuelan 747 grounded in Argentina
  • 'Guard cat' credited with preventing would-be robbery
  • UN says Yemen’s warring sides agree to renew existing truce
  • Guatemala arrests migrant smugglers wanted by the US
  • Justice Dept. sues Idaho over near-total abortion ban coming Aug. 25
  • Airbnb apologizes after Miss. ‘slave cabin’ is listed as luxury getaway
  • ‘Cooler than a Cadillac with AC in hundred-degree weather’
  • Kentucky judge reinstitutes state abortion ban, reversing lower court
  • Kansas voting in first referendum on abortion since Roe was overturned
  • 10-year-old is last Uvalde victim to leave the hospital, 66 days later
  • Beyoncé used ‘ableist’ slur in a new song. After uproar, she’s deleting it.
  • Phones of top Pentagon officials were wiped of Jan. 6 messages
  • Primaries live updates: Primaries in Mich., Mo., and Ariz. present a fresh test of Trump’s influence
  • Oath Keepers seditious conspiracy trial on track Sept. 26 as probes mount
  • Working from home during covid seems to work out for Biden
  • Memo shows Wis. GOP lawyer privately opposed decertifying Biden’s 2020 win
  • Post Politics Now: Biden says ‘justice has been delivered’ with killing of al-Qaeda’s Zawahiri
  • A Black woman hits glass ceiling then breaks ground as her own boss
  • Credit card debt surges as inflation pushes Americans to borrow more
  • Oatly, Premier Protein among 53 beverages recalled over contamination risk
  • Mo Ostin, who brought star musicians to Warner records, dies at 95
  • Job openings eased in June, reflecting early signs of slowdown
  • ‘MultiVersus’ is a real contender for Nintendo’s platform fighter crown
  • Electronics are built with death dates. Let’s not keep them a secret.
  • Jes Negrón’s first game after suing Riot is about heritage and a haunted house
  • D.C. townhouses away from the ‘hustle and bustle’
  • A personalized — and free — service for solving your DIY woes
  • Installing a floor is harder than you think. Here’s how to do it right.
  • Corporate Hope Cushions the Tech Sector, For Now
  • HSBC’s Promises Won’t Satisfy Ping An for Long
  • Uber Embodies the Post-Covid Inflationary Economy
  • Zawahiri Killing Was a Great Success of a Bygone Era
  • Understanding Monkeypox and How Outbreaks Spread
  • Why the World’s Oldest Sovereign Fund Is in Turmoil: QuickTake
  • How the Senate Strategy Known as Budget Reconciliation Works
  • Why Polio, Once Nearly Eradicated, Is Rebounding
  • Why I’m leading a congressional delegation to Taiwan
  • What $50 billion in taxpayer aid for airlines did not fix
  • The Taliban gave al-Qaeda a haven. Again.
  • Zawahiri’s death shows U.S. focus even two decades after 9/11
  • Facebook wants to be more like TikTok. That’s a bad idea.
  • Please, Tories. Don’t fall for the supply-side snake oil.
  • How the news business’s economics altered the news itself
  • The mini-Trumps are as big a threat to democracy as Trump is
  • 4 ways to improve the Manchin package
  • Biden might be the Democrats’ worst possible nominee (except for all the others)
  • The U.S. is bad at handling epidemics. Monkeypox is the latest example.
  • Our third party is not a party of ideas! It is the idea of a party!
  • The Electoral Count Reform Act must pass — even if it’s not perfect
  • The damage from Pelosi’s unwise Taiwan visit must be contained
  • If low-level zealots can be prosecuted for Jan. 6, so can Trump
  • DOJ’s new lawsuit against Idaho throws down the gauntlet on abortion
  • A promise Joe Biden should break
  • Poking the bear
  • Once united, British, U.S. and Canadian conservatives go their own ways
  • The real crisis over Taiwan will start after Pelosi comes home
  • The U.S. must act against extremism in Syrian camps
  • LIV golfers win unclean money
  • There’s nothing humorous about Samuel Alito’s comments
  • He who votes wins
  • Adoption should focus on the children
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 2 Aug, 2022

entry.content:

  • Kansas votes on abortion rights in US test case
CNA: Breaking News, Singapore News, World and Asia

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 2 Aug, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 2 Aug, 2022 published at 2022-08-02 23:15PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Top Hong Kong officials, politicians slam US Speaker Nancy Pelosi’s ‘extremely selfish’ visit to Taiwan, says it tramples on one-China policy
  • Mirror concert mishap: wife of second injured dancer speaks about moment giant screen fell during Hong Kong show
  • Hong Kong Housing Authority proposes 1.17 per cent rent hike for public flats
  • Hong Kong was ruled by Britain during occupation but was never its colony, education authorities say
  • Hong Kong Food Expo: new craft beers, old signatures but still no tasting allowed
  • HSBC executives face off with Hong Kong shareholders in charm offensive to fend off calls to split city’s largest bank
  • Firefighters called to douse flaming Ferrari sports car on Hong Kong highway
  • Hong Kong police seize HK$63 million worth of drugs, arrest 2 suspects trying to get into car used for storing cocaine
  • Hong Kong activist detained for inciting subversion of state wins judicial review to lift reporting restrictions on case
  • Hong Kong retail sales continue to decline, falling 1.2 per cent in June
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong approves Sinovac jab for toddlers as officials negotiate for BioNTech’s tailored vaccine formula
  • Amid Nancy Pelosi trip, Chinese ambassador warns UK lawmakers against Taiwan visit
  • China’s southern tech hub Shenzhen becomes first city on mainland to regulate fully autonomous, driverless cars on some roads
  • China, Philippines relaunch investment talks to reverse shortfall in Beijing’s pledged funding
  • US, global stock markets fall amid heightened US-China tensions
  • China vows military operations around Taiwan in response to US House Speaker Nancy Pelosi’s visit
  • Nancy Pelosi explains her Taiwan trip as vital to defence of democracy and freedom
  • Beijing condemns Nancy Pelosi’s trip to Taiwan, calling it ‘a serious violation’ of one-China policy
  • China’s property market slump and weak demand highlight fragile economic recovery
  • US House Speaker Nancy Pelosi arrives in Taiwan, defying Beijing’s warnings
  • Is US plan for ‘Middle East Quad’ another security challenge for China?
  • PLA ‘very likely’ to send warplanes over Taiwan Strait median line if Pelosi visits, analysts say
  • Chinese broadcaster CCTV says it ‘dares to fight Western media monopoly on public opinion’
  • Ant Group changes top management at Alipay corporate entity in latest restructuring move
  • China unveils system for filing antitrust reviews in sign of ‘normalised’ regulations in tech industry
  • China plans to view the universe’s dark past using a fleet of telescopes orbiting the moon
  • Face-to-face diplomacy crucial to steer US-China ties clear of conflict
  • China suspends imports of Taiwanese sweets, biscuits, pastries ahead of Nancy Pelosi’s likely visit
  • Chinese internet firm Sina, microblogging service Weibo abruptly close Taiwan platforms amid rising geopolitical tensions
  • Cross-strait tensions are bad news for Hong Kong, Chinese stocks no matter who’s bluffing in Taiwan spat
  • Taiwan Strait on the boil as US, Beijing and Taipei put military on high alert
  • Chinese army scrambles fighter jets across Taiwan Strait as US plane believed to carry Nancy Pelosi nears
  • Tencent and NetEase video game drought continues as China approves another 69 titles, mostly from small developers
  • Planes, ships and missile tests: how Beijing might react to Pelosi’s Taiwan trip
  • As tension soars, Taiwan prepares its air-raid shelters – from the subway to shopping malls
  • Why so much talk about Nancy Pelosi’s visit to Taiwan, and what does it mean for US-China relations?
  • Malaysia PM queries poor status in US human trafficking report with Nancy Pelosi amid Taiwan visit jitters
  • India raises spying fears as Chinese ship due to stop at Sri Lanka’s Hambantota Port
  • Singaporean accused of killing wife with pillow told UK police he wanted her to stop ‘nagging’
  • US used Pakistani airspace for drone that killed al-Qaeda chief Ayman al-Zawahri: analysts
  • Pakistan election agency rules former Prime Minister Imran Khan’s party received illegal funds
  • Australia fines traveller from Indonesia nearly US$2,000 for undeclared McMuffins: ‘Biosecurity is no joke’
  • Is South Korea’s Yoon Suk-yeol seeking to crush opponents with new police bureau?
  • US envoy to Japan seeks to counter China’s ‘economic coercion’ with technology alliance
  • North Korean hackers are posing as crypto freelancers to secure remote work abroad, researchers say
  • Singapore hawkers feel the sting of rising prices, ‘no thanks to Russia’
  • Philippines’ Fidel Ramos sought Beijing ties but also showed South China Sea defiance
  • Elizabeth Zhong murder: Chinese millionaire Fang Sun jailed for more than 18 years
  • First ship carrying Ukraine grain arrives in Türkiye
  • New York McDonald’s worker shot in face in argument over cold fries
  • Vladimir Putin’s rumoured girlfriend Alina Kabaeva hit with US sanctions over Ukraine war
  • UK man charged under Treason Act over crossbow threat to Queen Elizabeth
  • Ukraine war: Russia designates Azov Regiment steel plant defenders as terrorists
  • Russia says US directly involved in Ukraine war because American spies coordinating missile strikes on Moscow’s forces
  • Europe refugee crisis: record high 700 migrants crossed the English Channel to the UK by boat on a single day
  • Brittney Griner’s trial should be over ‘very soon’, lawyer says, as Russia warns ‘megaphone diplomacy’ would not secure prisoner swap
  • US accuses Russia of using Ukraine’s Zaporizhzhia power plant as ‘nuclear shield’
  • US watched al-Qaeda chief Zawahiri for months before ‘tailored air strike’ on Kabul hideout
  • Climate change: possible human extinction a ‘dangerously underexplored topic’, scientists say
  • Conjoined twins separated in 27-hour surgery with help of virtual reality
  • Did US use secret ‘ninja’ missile to kill al-Qaeda leader Zawahiri?
  • Big tech should share Europe network costs, say France, Italy, Spain, but some fear threat to net neutrality
  • Air China plans to raise US$2.2 billion through private share offering to buy new jets, replenish capital
  • Markets think they know how US, China central banks will respond to recession warnings – but do they?
  • Hong Kong developers exercise caution in pricing new projects after housing transactions fall for second month amid rising interest rates
  • Chinese stocks get cheaper for money managers who don’t want to be late for economy reopening party
  • What buyers of uncompleted properties overseas from Hong Kong should do to protect their interests
  • Alibaba, HSBC, AIA pace rout in Hong Kong on Taiwan tensions as China promises ‘firm and strong’ measures against Pelosi’s visit
  • Australia Foreign Investment Review Board fee hike is likely to weigh on home prices, property investment, analysts say
  • China’s state-run semiconductor fund engulfed in scandal as Beijing probes three senior executives for alleged corruption
  • Tech war: Trade friction, strict Covid-19 controls hinder inflow of overseas chip design talent
  • Foot care: the products, kit and precautions to take in summer to keep your feet looking and feeling great
  • After the NewJeans controversy, should K-pop groups stop debuting minors?
  • Disney+ K-drama Big Mouth: Lee Jong-suk returns as a cocky lawyer in brash but puzzling new series
  • The personal style that makes Martha Stewart a fashion influencer, and her streetwear line – she’s turning 81 on a roll
  • Far Far Away movie review: Hong Kong’s remote fringes and culture explored in Amos Why’s quirky romantic comedy
  • What are hiccups and how to make them stop? Eating sugar, drinking water backwards? Experts on their causes and cures
  • Tonga reopens borders after 2-year Covid-19 closure, and returning islanders cry tears of joy
  • Blackpink drop new song, announce upcoming album Born Pink and world tour, marking K-pop girl group’s long-awaited comeback
  • Chinese recipes and ‘stories of our culture’ in Chinese-Ish – but don’t think of it as a playbook for authentic Asian cuisine, say its co-authors
  • Hong Kong’s first hip-hop music awards to give recognition to city’s growing pool of rap talent
  • Meet Robert De Niro’s handsome son Julian De Niro, who plays a young Barack Obama in The First Lady and dines at New York’s Locanda Verde like Prince Harry and Meghan Markle
  • 6 most epic celebrity kids’ birthday parties, from Disney princesses for Cardi B and Offset’s daughter Kulture, to Saint West’s Jurassic Park bash thanks to Kim Kardashian and Kanye West
  • Meet the ‘Crocodile of Wall Street’, Heather Morgan: the crypto rapper and TikToker known as ‘Razzlekhan’ once allegedly laundered US$4.5 billion worth stolen from a Hong Kong bitcoin exchange
  • Inside Donnie Yen’s crazy rich life: as a Hong Kong martial arts icon and one of Asia’s highest-paid actors, the John Wick 4 actor splurges on BMWs, Rolexes – and a US$17 million mansion for his wife
  • What’s changed for Ermenegildo Zegna after going public? The Italian luxury fashion brand listed on the NYSE in 2021 … but remains very much a family company
  • Why is Hermès skipping out on the second-hand luxury market? Like LVMH, the Birkin bag maker is disinterested in sustainable, pre-owned fashion platforms
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 3 Aug, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Atomic-bombed violin traces family’s story of survival
  • Magnificent wooden canopy to be symbol of 2025 Osaka Expo

  • From subway stations to shopping malls, Taiwan prepares its air-raid shelters
  • Myanmar violence, Ukraine war loom over ASEAN meetings
  • Sri Lankans bide time as leaders seek fix for economic woes
  • U.N. chief warns world is one step from ‘nuclear annihilation’
  • Splash Mountain offers extra-wet summer version at Disneyland
  • JR Tokai runs its first hybrid train for tourists to Takayama
  • EDITORIAL: Stating case for clear, consistent messages about COVID-19 policy
  • VOX POPULI: Bribery scandal yet another stain on the business of the Olympics
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

entry.title: NY Times (Print): 2 Aug, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • U.S. Drone Strike Kills Ayman al-Zawahri, Top Qaeda Leader
